items.title: Structure
items.avoid: Parsing arguments at module top level.
items.prefer: Build the parser inside a main() guarded by if __name__ == '__main__'.
items.why: Keeps the module importable and testable.

items.title: Validation
items.avoid: Accepting raw strings and validating later.
items.prefer: Use type=, choices= and required= so argparse rejects bad input up front.
items.why: Fails fast with a clear usage message and exit code.
items.example: `add_argument('--mode', choices=['fast','accurate'], required=True)` — argparse rejects `--mode=quick` immediately with `error: argument --mode: invalid choice`.

items.title: Help text
items.prefer: Provide description and help= for each argument.
items.why: Generates a useful --help screen for users.
items.example:
```python
parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Chunk and embed documents.')
parser.add_argument('--max-tokens', type=int, default=512, help='max tokens per chunk (default: 512)')
```

items.title: Booleans
items.example: `add_argument('--no-color', action='store_true')` — presence = True, absence = False; no ambiguity about what string values mean.
